notes.author: Dr. Anna Reyes / Dr. Jennifer Beckman
notes.preview: 57-day admission discharge summary. 14 discharge diagnoses covering pancreatitis, ARDS, septic shock, DVT, AKI on CKD, cholelithiasis (surgery deferred), and chronic comorbidities. Discharge on apixaban + lisinopril + atorvastatin + acetaminophen + Ca/D + KCl + pantoprazole. Discharge to FutureCare Charles Village SNF for continued rehab. PCP, hematology, and surgery follow-ups arranged in Vietnamese.

notes.author: Dr. Anna Reyes / Dr. Jennifer Beckman
notes.preview: HD56. Pre-discharge progress note. PT cleared for SNF transfer functionally; ambulating 300 feet independently. Discharge tomorrow. Apixaban day 40 uneventful. Cr 1.3 (baseline). All meds reconciled. Vital signs stable.

notes.author: Jessica Park, RN, CCM / Diana Mercado, LCSW
notes.preview: HD53 family meeting (Vietnamese interpreter, 60 min) to finalize discharge: FutureCare Charles Village SNF 4/12. Family caregiver education. Med rec preliminary. Outpatient follow-ups booked (PCP 5/2, heme 5/15, surgery 6/1). Healthcare proxy formalized (son).

notes.author: Dr. Anna Reyes / Dr. Jennifer Beckman
notes.preview: HD47. SNF placement delayed — Bayview rehab bed unavailable; pursuing FutureCare Charles Village. Family prefers latter (closer + some Vietnamese-speaking staff). Patient functionally improving: 200 feet with walker, no assist. BP slightly elevated (138/80).

notes.author: Dr. Marcus Hong / Dr. Jennifer Beckman
notes.preview: HD34. Floor day 8. PT/OT progress noted. Bayview Acute Rehab accepted, bed anticipated 4/2-4/5. General Surgery counseled: defer chole to outpatient. Hgb improving.

notes.type: Step-Down to Medicine Floor Transfer Summary
notes.author: Dr. Marcus Hong / Dr. Jennifer Beckman
notes.preview: HD26 transfer from step-down to medicine floor. Summary of ICU course (sepsis/ARDS/intubation 2/17-2/27), RLE DVT 3/2 on apixaban, AKI resolved, severe deconditioning, restarting lisinopril and atorvastatin. PT/OT continuing, rehab placement planned.

notes.author: Dr. Allyson Pishko / Dr. Maya Singh
notes.preview: HD16. Acute proximal RLE DVT diagnosed (R common femoral through popliteal). Initiated heparin gtt, plan transition to apixaban 10 mg BID x 7d then 5 mg BID. Provoked VTE: prolonged ICU, immobility, severe pancreatitis. Goal 3 months minimum. Hematology follow-up arranged.

notes.author: Dr. Sarah Patel / Dr. Daniel Foster
notes.preview: HD8. ARDS improving (P/F 168); vent weaning ongoing. AFB smears x 3 + NAAT all negative — active TB ruled out, airborne isolation discontinued. Pip/tazo continuing. Cr improving 1.6. Floor transfer anticipated when extubated.

notes.author: Dr. Lisa Tran / Dr. Hassan Nasser
notes.preview: HD5 ID consult. (1) Antibiotic optimization for biliary E. coli sepsis (continue pip/tazo, stop vanco — admission CoNS contaminant). (2) Rule out active TB given LTBI history + Vietnamese origin + pulmonary nodule. Airborne isolation; 3 AFB smears + 1 NAAT ordered.

notes.author: Dr. Rebecca Chen / Dr. Anil Vachani
notes.preview: HD4. Pulm consult for moderate ARDS (P/F 140) management AND interpretation of incidental 2.5 cm RUL pulmonary nodule on serial CXRs. Recommend dedicated chest CT once stable for transport; rule out active TB now.

notes.author: Dr. Sarah Patel / Dr. Daniel Foster
notes.preview: HD2. Transfer from medicine floor to MICU for worsening respiratory failure (HFNC 6L), hypotension despite resuscitation, presumed severe biliary sepsis. Status post emergent ERCP today: sphincterotomy + stone extraction + biliary pus drained. Norepi 0.02 mcg/kg/min. AM CXR: bilateral interstitial markings + small bilateral pleural effusions + incidental 2.5 cm RUL nodule (radiologist recommends dedicated CT chest).

notes.author: Dr. Marcus Hong / Dr. Jennifer Beckman / Dr. Kavita Patel
notes.preview: Admission H&P. 72F Vietnamese-speaking with cholelithiasis, CKD III, HTN, HLD presents with severe RUQ/epigastric pain, fevers, hypotension after fatty Tết meal. Lipase 603; LFTs with hyperbilirubinemia (T-bili 3.2); WBC 17.3; AKI on CKD (Cr 1.8 from baseline 1.3). CT A/P: peripancreatic stranding, cholelithiasis, intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. Started pip/tazo, IVF resuscitation, GI for ERCP.

notes.author: Dr. Phong Le
notes.department: Primary Care (Charm City Asian Health Center)
notes.preview: Most recent annual visit. HTN/HLD at goal. CKD stage 3a stable (Cr 1.3, eGFR 47). Cholelithiasis — surgery deferred by patient. Brother died of lung cancer 3/2026. Shared decision-making about LDCT screening; patient declined again (cost). Continues NSAID use despite CKD counseling. Flu shot.

notes.author: Dr. Sandra Kim
notes.preview: Biliary colic. Two RUQ pain episodes after fatty meals. Abdominal US: multiple gallstones in distended GB, no acute cholecystitis. LFTs/lipase normal. Referred to General Surgery for elective lap chole; low-fat diet.

notes.author: Dr. Phong Le
notes.department: Primary Care (Charm City Asian Health Center)
notes.preview: Annual visit. Newly recognized CKD stage 2/3a (Cr 1.2, eGFR 47, 1+ proteinuria). NSAID counseling. Older brother just diagnosed with advanced lung cancer in Vietnam. Discussion of lung cancer screening — does not meet USPSTF; patient considering.

notes.author: Dr. Marcus Reilly
notes.preview: Pre-op evaluation for right TKA. KL grade 4 medial-compartment OA. Decade of conservative management exhausted. Surgery scheduled 4/12/2018.

notes.author: Dr. Phong Le
notes.department: Primary Care (Charm City Asian Health Center)
notes.preview: Annual visit. HTN well-controlled. New HLD (LDL 168) — started atorvastatin. Smoking cessation confirmed (quit 2004, ~14 pack-years lifetime). Latent TB infection — declined INH treatment again. Continuing 1–2 drinks/day.

notes.author: Dr. Karen O'Sullivan
notes.preview: Outpatient evaluation of positive PPD (14 mm) + positive QuantiFERON in a Vietnamese-born adult. CXR clear; sputum AFBs negative; cultures pending. Latent TB infection diagnosed. INH offered; patient declined given alcohol/monitoring burden.

notes.author: Dr. Phong Le
notes.department: Primary Care (Charm City Asian Health Center)
notes.preview: Establishing care after workplace TB screening returned positive (PPD 14 mm). Vietnamese immigrant, 1990. Newly identified HTN (BP 142/86). Current smoker 10 cig/day. Right knee OA suspected. Referred to ID/Pulm for LTBI workup.

_status: notes complete (19 total: 7 historical + 12 admission course). Labs, imaging, problems, meds, vitals indices to be added in Chunk 4.
